newsletters.subject: Ben's Builds #3 - an email app
newsletters.relevance_reason: Detailed case study of building a local email app using AI coding agents (Codex for initial pass, Factory for iteration between Claude Opus and GPT-5.5). Covers agent-friendly UI design, iterative agent-driven development workflow, practical challenges (lag, caching, rule sync with Gmail API). Directly relevant to coding agents, agent UX design, and AI-assisted development workflows.

wiki_actions.details: Update Ben's Bites entity with details about Ben's Builds series and his approach to agent-driven development
Extract patterns: hidden selectors/state/debug endpoints for agents, optimistic UI with agent-readable state, local-first app with Gmail API backend
Add real-world example of Codex → Factory handoff pattern for multi-agent development

newsletters.subject: The Man Who Studied Power and Then Built It: Alex Karp, Palantir, and The Technological Republic
newsletters.relevance_reason: Profile/interview piece about Alex Karp and Palantir's book 'The Technological Republic'. Primarily business/philosophy content about Palantir's company culture, defense AI, and Western values. Limited direct relevance to LLM/Agent technology wiki. Some tangential mentions of LLMs as 'management of resources' but not technical depth.

wiki_actions.details: Create/update entity page for Alex Karp with focus on his views on AI/ML in defense and enterprise, LLM-as-management-layer thesis
Reference Karp's stated views on LLMs and AI strategy at Palantir

recommended_next_steps: Process Ben's Builds article into wiki pages on agent-driven development workflows
Scrape the Palantir/Karp article for any technical AI content to enrich entity pages
Consider tracking Ben's Bites as a recurring source for coding agent case studies
